top_categories.workout_context: 6,562ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Niseko Classic's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
At mile 60 of 87, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
75ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 87 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Niseko Classic. Train it until it feels boring.
Niseko Classic's mountain passes forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 87 miles.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 6,562ft of climbing on mountain passes does to your power file on race day.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 87 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
At Niseko Classic, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Tempo is Niseko Classic's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 87 miles on mountain passes. Train it until it's automatic.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mountain passes at tempo for 87 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Niseko Classic’s 87 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Niseko Classic with 6,562ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 87 miles of mountain passes in Niseko, Hokkaido, Japan.
